title: OpenClaw v2026.5.19: Global Skills, Docker PIP & 150 Fixes
description: OpenClaw v2026.5.19 ships the --global skills flag for enterprise teams, Docker Python package support, URL allowlists, and 150+ channel reliability fixes for WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, and Discord.
author: PulseAgent
content:
# OpenClaw v2026.5.19: Global Skills, Docker PIP & 150 Fixes

OpenClaw v2026.5.19 landed on May 20, 2026 — a focused reliability and enterprise-tooling release that ships **150+ bug fixes**, a new `--global` flag for shared skill management, Docker Python package installation at build time, and tighter browser security controls. If you run multi-agent B2B sales workflows at scale, this update directly addresses pain points in skill distribution, channel reliability, and containerized deployment.

## What's New in v2026.5.19

### Global Skills Flag: One Install, All Agents

The most impactful addition for teams managing multiple OpenClaw deployments is the new `--global` flag on `openclaw skills install` and `openclaw skills update`:

```bash
openclaw skills install b2b-sdr-agent --global
openclaw skills update autoreview --global
```

Before this, skills had to be installed per workspace. With `--global`, a skill installed once becomes available to every agent running on that node — eliminating duplicated setup across multi-tenant or multi-agent configurations. For B2B teams running separate agents per product line, region, or channel, this cuts onboarding time from hours to minutes.

> **Try it now**: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template/main/install.sh | bash`

### Docker Python Package Support

v2026.5.19 adds `OPENCLAW_IMAGE_PIP_PACKAGES` to complement the existing `OPENCLAW_IMAGE_APT_PACKAGES` build argument:

```dockerfile
docker build \
  --build-arg OPENCLAW_IMAGE_APT_PACKAGES="libpq-dev ffmpeg" \
  --build-arg OPENCLAW_IMAGE_PIP_PACKAGES="pandas requests-html pypdf2" \
  .
```

This unlocks Python-native workflows inside OpenClaw containers without custom base images — useful for skills that process PDFs, call Python ML models, scrape structured data, or integrate with Python-first APIs. B2B export teams can now bundle data-enrichment pipelines directly into their agent image.

### 150+ Fixes: Channel Reliability at Scale

This release addresses **150+ bugs** across every major channel — double the 74+ fixes in v2026.5.18. The highlights most relevant to B2B sales automation:

**WhatsApp:**
- Forced document delivery for media sends now honored — attachments (product catalogs, contracts, datasheets) arrive as files, not inline media thumbnails.

**Telegram:**
- Forum topics no longer block sibling topic traffic — parallel lead conversations in different topics stay independent.
- Streamed reply previews preserved with tool-warning finals — leads see real-time typing indicators throughout multi-step responses.

**Slack:**
- Delivered inbound message IDs persisted — prevents duplicate reply sends under high-traffic conditions.

**Discord:**
- Streamed reply previews preserved with tool-warning final messages — consistent experience across your full channel stack.

**Cross-channel stability:**
- Port validation now accepts values above 65535 (regression fix — had been silently breaking non-standard port configs).
- Memory search scan optimized to prevent multi-second Node.js main thread pins during semantic search calls.
- Session rotation fixed when transcript files are missing — agents no longer stall on first-boot in clean environments.
- DeepSeek MCP tool schemas normalized with `anyOf`/`oneOf` unions — fixes tool routing for DeepSeek-backed agents.
- Google Gemini tool-call thought signatures recovered during native replay.
- OpenAI deterministic tool payload ordering preserved for prompt-cache reuse — reduces token cost on repeated similar calls.

### Browser Security: URL Allowlists for /act and /highlight

URL allowlist enforcement is now active for the `/act` and `/highlight` browser routes. This matters for B2B teams using OpenClaw's browser automation for lead research, CRM scraping, or web form submission — you can now explicitly declare which domains agents are permitted to interact with, blocking accidental or malicious navigation to off-scope URLs.

Also new: `openclaw browser evaluate --timeout-ms` lets you set per-evaluation execution budgets, preventing runaway scripts from stalling agent turns during heavy web automation tasks.

### QA-Lab: Enterprise-Grade Parity Testing

v2026.5.19 ships new QA-lab scenarios for enterprise validation:

```bash
openclaw qa suite --runtime-parity-tier first-hour
openclaw qa coverage --tools
```

The `--runtime-parity-tier first-hour` scenario runs 20-turn and optional 100-turn conversation parity tests, validating that your deployment behaves consistently across the first hour of a session — critical for long B2B discovery calls where context must persist correctly.

`openclaw qa coverage --tools` reports fixture coverage across all tool calls, helping ops teams identify undertested tool paths before production incidents occur.

## Impact for B2B Sales Teams Using PulseAgent

If you're running [PulseAgent](https://pulseagent.io/app/login?ref=blog&utm_source=blog&utm_medium=release-post&utm_campaign=openclaw-v2026.5.19) on top of OpenClaw, v2026.5.19 delivers measurable improvements across your entire deployment:

| Area | v2026.5.18 | v2026.5.19 |
|------|-----------|------------|
| Skills management | Per-workspace only | `--global` flag for node-wide shared skills |
| Docker customization | APT packages only | APT + PIP packages |
| Bug fixes shipped | 74+ | 150+ |
| WhatsApp doc delivery | Inconsistent | Forced document delivery honored |
| Browser security | Open navigation | URL allowlist enforcement |
| QA tooling | Basic | Runtime parity + tool coverage reports |

The WhatsApp document delivery fix is a significant win — sales reps frequently reported that product catalogs sent via WhatsApp arrived as media thumbnails instead of downloadable files. That regression is now resolved.

For teams running [AI SDR for B2B export](/solutions/ai-sdr-for-b2b-export), the global skills flag means you can deploy your prospecting, follow-up, and closing skills once and have them available across all your regional agents instantly. For [WhatsApp sales automation](/solutions/whatsapp-sales-automation) specifically, the document delivery fix ensures your product brochures and quotation PDFs land correctly in every conversation.

## How to Upgrade

```bash
# One-command install/upgrade
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iPythoning/b2b-sdr-agent-template/main/install.sh | bash

# Or update directly
openclaw update
```

**Breaking change:** Node.js 22.19 is now the minimum required version. Check your runtime before upgrading:

```bash
node --version  # Must be >= 22.19
```

If you're on an older version, update Node.js before upgrading OpenClaw to avoid startup errors.

## FAQ

**Q: Does `--global` work with PulseAgent's multi-channel setup?**
Yes. Skills installed globally are available to all agents on the same node, including separate WhatsApp, Telegram, and Slack agents running in parallel.

**Q: Do I need to rebuild my Docker image to use PIP packages?**
Yes. `OPENCLAW_IMAGE_PIP_PACKAGES` is a build-time argument — you'll need to rebuild your container image with the new `--build-arg`.

**Q: What's the impact of the memory search optimization?**
The fix prevents Node.js main thread pins that caused 2–5 second response delays during semantic memory lookups. High-volume B2B deployments with large conversation histories will see the most benefit.

**Q: Is v2026.5.19 safe to deploy without staging?**
We recommend running `openclaw qa suite --runtime-parity-tier first-hour` against your staging environment before promoting to production, especially if you rely on long-running conversation sessions.

**Q: What does the port validation fix affect?**
It corrects a regression where port numbers above 65535 were rejected, breaking non-standard port configurations silently. If you use custom port assignments in your gateway config, this fix prevents unexpected failures.

---

OpenClaw v2026.5.19 is a quality release with outsized impact for teams operating at scale. The `--global` skills flag alone simplifies enterprise deployments significantly, and 150 fixes across all major channels means more reliable conversations with your leads every day.
